latestInfos.content:
Proposals Numbers

Call ERASMUS-EDU-2022-ECHE-CERT, the last cut off closed on the 24/03/2026 17:00.

559 proposals have been submitted.

Evaluation results are expected to be communicated at the end of September 2026.

Call Update – Erasmus Charter for Higher Education (ECHE)

The call text has been updated to reflect the following changes:

-Postponement of cut off deadline from 27/01/2026 to 24/03/2026.

-Eligibility has been extended to accommodate the potential participation of the United Kingdom in the Erasmus+ Programme.

EVALUATION results

Deadline: 28.01.2025

The results of the evaluation are as follows:



Number of proposals submitted

336

Number of inadmissible proposals

4

Number of ineligible proposals

40

Number of above-threshold proposals

235

Number of below threshold proposals

47

We recently informed the applicants about the evaluation results for their proposals.

Call Update – Erasmus Charter for Higher Education (ECHE)

The call text has been updated to reflect the following changes:

·      The final cut-off date of 26 January 2027 has been removed.

·      Eligibility has been extended to accommodate the potential participation of Switzerland in the Erasmus+ Programme.

·      The reference to the EU Financial Regulation has been updated to Regulation (EU) 2024/2509 (“recast”).

EVALUATION results

Deadline: 25.01.2024

The results of the evaluation are as follows:

 

  

Number of proposals submitted

431

Number of inadmissible proposals

8

Number of ineligible proposals

43

Number of above-threshold proposals

289

Number of below threshold proposals

71

We recently informed the applicants about the evaluation results for their proposals.

 

The list of Higher education institutions holding an ECHE - 2021-2027 is available here

 

 https://erasmus-plus.ec.europa.eu/document/higher-education-institutions-holding-an-eche-2021-2027

PROPOSAL NUMBERS 

Call ERASMUS-EDU-2022-ECHE-CERT, the third cut off closed on the 25/01/2024 17:00.

431 proposals have been submitted.

Evaluation results are expected to be communicated at the end of June 2024.

INFORMATION ON APPLICATIONS RECEIVED

Call ERASMUS-EDU-2022_ECHE_CERT-FP Erasmus Charter for Higher Education: the second cut off closed on 26 Juanuary 2023.

339 proposals were submitted.                                                                                

Evaluation results are expected to be communicated at the beginning of July 2023.

EVALUATION results

The results of the evaluation for the first cut-off are as follows:

We recently informed the applicants about the evaluation results for their proposals.

 

For questions, please contact EACEA-ECHE@ec.europa.eu;

programmeDivision.description: Quality Label for humanitarian aid organisations - FP

latestInfos.content:
Call ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL - Quality Label Humanitarian Aid has closed on the 21 September 2023 at 17:00:00 (Brussels time).

49 proposals have been submitted. The results will be available on February 2024.

 

The new submission session is now available for: ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL.

Next deadline is 24 September 2024 at 17:00:00 (Brussels time).
The submission session is now available for: ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-FP(ESC-CERT), ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-LP(ESC-CERT)

Call ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL - Quality Label Humanitarian Aid has closed on the 22 September 2022 at 17:00:00 (Brussels time).

49 proposals have been submitted.

The breakdown per topic is:

·       ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-LP - Quality Label Humanitarian Aid - Light Procedure 12 proposals

·       ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-FP - Quality Label Humanitarian Aid - Full Procedure: 37 proposals

Evaluation results are expected to be communicated in February 2023.
The submission session is now available for: ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-FP(ESC-CERT)

PROPOSAL NUMBERS

Call ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL has closed on the 24 March 2022.

24 proposals have been submitted.                                                                 

The breakdown per topic is:

  • ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-FP : 11 proposals
  • ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-LP: 13 proposals

 

Evaluation results are expected to be communicated in September 2022.
The submission session is now available for: ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-FP(ESC-CERT), ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-LP(ESC-CERT)

120 proposals have been submitted.

The breakdown per topic is:

  • ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-LP - Quality Label Humanitarian Aid - Light Procedure: 89 proposals

Evaluation results are expected to be communicated in February 2022.

120 proposals have been submitted.

The breakdown per topic is:

  • ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-LP - Quality Label Humanitarian Aid - Light Procedure 89 proposals
  • ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-FP - Quality Label Humanitarian Aid - Full Procedure: 31 proposals

Evaluation results are expected to be communicated in February 2022.

All applicants with branch offices have to fill in the Annex Tpl List of Branches.

The answers to the questions related to the award criteria for Branches have to be filled in part B of the application, where applicants have to provide information for each of the branch offices and specify if they hold or not a valid Certification. Applicants applying for the Light Procedure, with branch offices certified under EUAV Initiative have to answer to the award criteria questions in Part B following the instructions for light procedure. For any branch offices not certified under EUAV, the answers have to be provided following the instructions for the full procedure.

The excel table [Tpl_List of Branches (ESC HUMAID Quality Label] to be downloaded on the Platform with Part B has to be filled in for all branches (simplified hosting organisations), on behalf of which the support organisation applies.

All applicants with branch offices have to fill in the Annex Tpl List of Branches.

The answers to the questions related to the award criteria for Branches have to be filled in part B of the application, where applicants have to provide information for each of the branch offices and specify if they hold or not a valid Certification. Applicants applying for the Light Procedure, with branch offices certified under EUAV Initiative have to answer to the award criteria questions in Part B following the instructions for light procedure. For any branch offices not certified under EUAV, the answers have to be provided following the instructions for the full procedure.

The excel table [Tpl_List of Branches (ESC HUMAID Quality Label] to be downloaded on the Platform with Part B has to be filled in for all branches (simplified hosting organisations), on behalf of which the support organisation applies.
The submission session is now available for: ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-FP(ESC-CERT), ESC-HUMAID-2021-QUAL-LABEL-LP(ESC-CERT)

GrantTenderObj.title: Strengthening the capacities of the National institutions in environmental sector for infrastructure projects management
GrantTenderObj.descriptionTender: Contract shall provide technical support to the IPA units of the Ministry of Environmental Protection (MEP) and Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Water Management (MAFWM) as well as to end recipients of the projects (e.g. Local Self-Government Units and their Public Utility Companies, regional companies, Public Water Management Companies) to successfully perform their respective roles in indirect management of EU funded projects, regarding to preparation and implementation of investment projects in the environmental protection sector.

GrantTenderObj.title: Interpreting Services
GrantTenderObj.descriptionTender: The objective of the call for tenders is to provide high-quality interpreting services for meetings at the Council and the Committee of Regions, subject to the conditions laid down in these technical specifications.The contractor must be prepared to provide the contracting authority with the full range of services listed in the tender specifications. The contractor should be aware that, depending on the nature of the event and/or specific organisational conditions, the planning and arrangements for the interpreting services may change rapidly.

lots.title: Lot 1 General Secretariat of the Council, Interpreting Services
lots.description: The contractor must ensure that it has sufficient capacity to:• ensure interpreting services for approximately 260 meetings per year;• perform in Brussels (around 240 meetings per year, with various language regimes), in Luxembourg (around 10 Council meetings per year), and worldwide (around 10 meetings with various language regimes);• ensure interpreting services for full-day meetings (around 95 % of the meetings covered by this call for tenders) or half-day meetings (around 5 % of meetings);• perform all types of interpreting (simultaneous, consecutive, and whispering);• use the interpreting facilities provided by the GSC: fixed booths, mobile booths, portable interpreting systems and videoconferencing platforms;• cover the GSC’s need for interpreting as requested, including in the exceptional cases where services are required at night, at weekends or on public holidays;• provide interpreting services occasionally at very short notice;• interpret political discussions on complex technical matters;• treat information with confidentiality and discretion.

lots.title: Lot 2 European Committee of the Regions, Interpreting Services
lots.description: Contractors must be able to provide, upon request, interpreting services in all the languages specified.The languages most frequently requested are the 24 EU languages.In addition, in the case of meetings with non-EU participants, the most frequently used languages for the meetings covered by this call for tenders are: Albanian, Arabic, Bosnian, Macedonian, Montenegrin, Russian, Serbian, Turkish and Ukrainian.In exceptional cases, Catalan, Valencian, Basque and Galician may be requested.The contracting authority may also request additional non-EU languages.The contractor should ensure that it has sufficient capacity to:• provide interpreting services for approximately 120 meetings per year;• perform in Brussels (around 90 meetings per year, with various language regimes) and in the EU Member States (around 30 meetings with various language regimes);• provide interpreting services for full-day meetings (around 80 % of the meetings) or half-day meetings (around 20 % of the meetings);• provide the interpreting services on the CoR’s premises and on other premises in Belgium and in EU Member States, for in-person and hybrid meetings (for the participants);• perform all types of interpreting (simultaneous, consecutive and whispering);• use the interpreting facilities provided by the CoR: fixed booths, mobile booths and portable interpreting systems;• cover the CoR’s need for interpreting as requested, including exceptional requests for interpreting services needed at night, at weekends or on public holidays;• provide interpreting services occasionally at very short notice;• interpret political discussions on complex technical matters;• treat information with confidentiality and discretion.

GrantTenderObj.title: Outreach to and engagement with young people in EU External Action
GrantTenderObj.descriptionTender: Support to increase the impact and sustainability of outreach to and engagement with young people in EU External Action. Tasks include (but are not limited to) curating information on opportunities for young people in external action, supporting young people in benefiting from these opportunities (e.g., through online learning activities or peer exchanges) and developing engaging actions (e.g., consultations, training, identification of programs). The consultant would also support community management, and create and implement a strategy for engagement with alumni.

GrantTenderObj.title: Support to ERERA in its mission to implement effective regulatory mechanisms contributing to the development of a regional electricity market
GrantTenderObj.descriptionTender: Support to ERERA in its mission to implement effective regulatory mechanisms contributing to the development of a regional electricity market
